first_five_rebuild_work_packages.proof: Postgres-oriented schema, tenant isolation policy, domain event list, seed fixture, and JSON-to-table migration map are generated by scripts/run_production_data_model.py

Packet state, events, eight packet views, six scores, source routes, blockers, and next moves are generated from real local packet/evidence/source/review/report artifacts by scripts/run_production_packet_engine.py

Canada, India, Vietnam demo, and generic fallback country packs, source lifecycle rows, source freshness states, packet impacts, and claim gates are generated from the official source registry and dated source-refresh records by scripts/run_production_country_source_engine.py

Market metrics, source routes, dataset connector states, capped market score, blocked demand/profit/buyer claims, and next validation moves are generated from the packet and official source registry by scripts/run_production_market_intelligence_engine.py
report requirements keep claim boundaries, citations, evidence links, versions, and watermarks

packet_state_machine.claim_rule: State progress never opens customs, tariff, buyer, supplier, shipment, legal, payment, or public-launch claims without evidence gates.

production_layers.name: Product doctrine
production_layers.purpose: Lock what the product is and is not.

production_layers.name: Domain model
production_layers.purpose: Make all product behavior map to durable entities.

production_layers.name: Data platform
production_layers.purpose: Move JSON/SQLite proof into governed production persistence.

production_layers.name: Workflow engine
production_layers.purpose: Run packets through explicit lifecycle states and review gates.

production_layers.name: Country-pack engine
production_layers.purpose: Scale country-specific routing and claim boundaries.

production_layers.name: Source intelligence engine
production_layers.purpose: Track official-source permissions, freshness, diffs, and packet impact.

production_layers.name: Market intelligence engine
production_layers.purpose: Produce source-backed research signals without demand claims.

production_layers.name: Evidence and document intelligence engine
production_layers.purpose: Extract, confirm, and govern evidence from files.

production_layers.name: Decision and rules engine
production_layers.purpose: Explain capped scores and blocked claims.

production_layers.name: AI copilot layer
production_layers.purpose: Use AI for assistance, never approval.

production_layers.name: Expert review network
production_layers.purpose: Turn human review into product workflow.

production_layers.name: User portals
production_layers.purpose: Give each user type a complete workflow.

production_layers.name: Enterprise platform
production_layers.purpose: Support firms, teams, clients, APIs, audit, and retention controls.

production_layers.name: Production operations and go-live system
production_layers.purpose: Operate only approved scopes with observable controls.

proof_boundary: This artifact completes the local production redevelopment contract. It does not prove production deployment, legal/privacy/security approval, qualified customs/trade review, buyer/supplier validation, live payments, real user outcomes, or public go/no-go approval.

redevelopment_phases.proof: Production document intelligence now maps declared document gaps, downloaded official sample forms, source-route-only country samples, synthetic filled parser QA documents, extracted-field provenance, redaction previews, and evidence records while keeping real upload/security/AI/document claims closed.

redevelopment_phases.name: Evidence ledger and claim-gate engine
redevelopment_phases.next_valid_move: Build the production implementation for this phase and attach the listed evidence before any gated claim can open.
redevelopment_phases.proof: can_show_claim decisions, evidence trails, required evidence types, stale/reference-only evidence blockers, and claim/evidence mapper rows are generated from local packet, source, market, document, and review artifacts by scripts/run_production_evidence_claim_gate_engine.py while external claims remain closed.

redevelopment_phases.name: Decision and scoring engine
redevelopment_phases.next_valid_move: Build the production implementation for this phase and attach the listed evidence before any gated claim can open.
redevelopment_phases.proof: Six separate score records, threshold bands, cap policies, claim-gate dependencies, evidence references, blocker fields, reasons, and next actions are generated from local packet, business, market, document, and claim-gate artifacts by scripts/run_production_decision_scoring_engine.py without creating a single readiness score or approval language.

redevelopment_phases.name: AI copilot layer
redevelopment_phases.next_valid_move: Build the production implementation for this phase and attach the listed evidence before any gated claim can open.
redevelopment_phases.proof: Eight AI role contracts, output labels, permission/redaction/manual fallback requirements, prompt-injection safety checks, and fail-closed output contracts are generated from local AI policy, model router, redaction, no-AI, document, and claim-gate artifacts by scripts/run_production_ai_copilot_engine.py with live model calls and gate-opening claims closed.

redevelopment_phases.name: Expert review network
redevelopment_phases.next_valid_move: Build the production implementation for this phase and attach the listed evidence before any gated claim can open.
redevelopment_phases.proof: Ten reviewer lanes, credential requirements, scoped review requests, finding templates, gate-impact rows, and audit events are generated by scripts/run_production_expert_review_network.py. Real reviewer credentials and signed findings remain missing, no completed signoff is recorded, and external claim gates stay closed.

redevelopment_phases.name: Reports and deliverables engine
redevelopment_phases.next_valid_move: Build the production implementation for this phase and attach the listed evidence before any gated claim can open.
redevelopment_phases.proof: Twelve report types are exported as JSON, HTML, and PDF from packet, evidence, source, score, claim-gate, and review artifacts by scripts/run_production_reports_engine.py. Every report has citations, version, draft watermark, review status, and a blocked-claims section; no report opens external claims.

redevelopment_phases.name: User portals and workflows
redevelopment_phases.next_valid_move: Build the production implementation for this phase and attach the listed evidence before any gated claim can open.
redevelopment_phases.proof: Six portal workflow records, four default first-screen options, route coverage, UX checks, and closed portal gate controls are generated from the real runtime UI/API route map by scripts/run_production_portal_workflow_engine.py. Real UX test results, accessibility signoff, hosted proof, unrestricted uploads, live payment, and public launch remain closed.

redevelopment_phases.name: Enterprise SaaS and API platform
redevelopment_phases.next_valid_move: Build the production implementation for this phase and attach the listed evidence before any gated claim can open.
redevelopment_phases.proof: Seventeen enterprise API contracts, organization/workspace controls, RBAC permission matrix, API-key fingerprints, webhook contracts, usage limits, audit export rules, and white-label report policy are generated by scripts/run_production_enterprise_api_platform.py from runtime/auth/data/report/claim-gate artifacts. Hosted auth, live API keys, webhook delivery, unrestricted uploads, enterprise terms, security review, and public launch remain closed.

redevelopment_phases.name: Payments and monetization
redevelopment_phases.next_valid_move: Build the production implementation for this phase and attach the listed evidence before any gated claim can open.
redevelopment_phases.proof: Seven pricing tiers, allowed paid scope, forbidden paid scope, Stripe/payment research references, checkout controls, webhook controls, and ten blocked payment gates are generated by scripts/run_production_payment_monetization_engine.py from billing controls and the payment-pricing contract. Live checkout, live payment objects, external charges, webhook delivery, tax/accounting approval, refund/support approval, payment security approval, and claim-language approval remain closed.

redevelopment_phases.name: Security, privacy, reliability, and production trust
redevelopment_phases.next_valid_move: Build the production implementation for this phase and attach the listed evidence before any gated claim can open.
redevelopment_phases.proof: Fifteen production trust controls, vendor/data-processing review records, incident runbooks, official security/privacy/AI research references, and a real local backup/restore hash drill are generated by scripts/run_production_security_privacy_reliability_engine.py from local auth/RBAC/upload/AI/audit/deletion/runtime artifacts. Managed auth, admin MFA, hosted sessions, private object storage, malware scanning, retention approval, vendor approval, production backup restore, monitoring, incident rehearsal, secrets, data-residency approval, real file uploads, hosted private beta, and public launch remain closed.

redevelopment_phases.name: Launch control plane
redevelopment_phases.next_valid_move: Build the production implementation for this phase and attach the listed evidence before any gated claim can open.
redevelopment_phases.proof: Thirteen launch gates, six candidate public-scope items, eight blocked public-scope items, source artifact evidence, and final activation decision records are generated by scripts/run_production_launch_control_plane.py from business, country/source, market, security/privacy, AI, claim-gate, expert-review, payment, private-beta, deployment, and final-go-live artifacts. Exact public scope, activation, hosted private beta, live payment, real uploads, external claims, and public launch remain blocked.

research_anchors.claim_boundary: Supports research routing only; it does not validate buyer demand or profitability.
research_anchors.usable_fact: Trade Data Online is the Canada trade data route for import/export market and competition research.

research_anchors.claim_boundary: Supports market-access comparison routing only; qualified review is required before tariff or market-access claims.
research_anchors.source_name: International Trade Centre Market Access Map
research_anchors.usable_fact: Market Access Map is the market-access route for tariffs, trade measures, regulatory requirements, and preference comparisons.

research_anchors.claim_boundary: Routes users to official and qualified review paths; the product does not replace CBSA or a customs professional.
research_anchors.source_name: Canada Border Services Agency commercial import guidance
research_anchors.usable_fact: CBSA is the official routing source for commercial importing topics such as business/import accounts, tariff resources, other government requirements, origin, rulings, and accounting.

research_anchors.claim_boundary: Reference route only; importers and reviewers must confirm current requirements for the exact product and import time.
research_anchors.usable_fact: AIRS is the CFIA reference route for food, plant, animal, and regulated-commodity import requirements.

research_anchors.claim_boundary: Security implementation guidance; hosted readiness still requires product-specific security review.
research_anchors.usable_fact: File-upload security needs defense in depth: extension/type validation, generated filenames, size limits, authorization, isolated storage, malware checks, updated libraries, and CSRF protection.

research_anchors.claim_boundary: Governance reference only; it does not certify the product or approve AI outputs.
research_anchors.usable_fact: NIST AI RMF is the AI governance route for trustworthy AI design, development, use, and evaluation.

research_anchors.claim_boundary: Payment implementation reference only; live checkout remains disabled until payment review and production setup are complete.
research_anchors.usable_fact: Payment launch must cover live/test mode differences, API keys, live-mode objects, webhooks, edge cases, logging, and duplicate/delayed/out-of-order events.

research_anchors.claim_boundary: Privacy design input only; legal/privacy approval requires qualified review for the product and data flows.
research_anchors.source_name: Office of the Privacy Commissioner of Canada PIPEDA principles
research_anchors.usable_fact: Canada privacy governance should account for accountability, purpose identification, consent, limiting collection/use/retention, safeguards, openness, access, and challenge rights.

research_anchors.claim_boundary: Tariff classification and treatment remain blocked until qualified review or authoritative ruling evidence exists.
research_anchors.usable_fact: The Canadian Customs Tariff is the source route for Canadian tariff orientation and HS candidate research.

research_anchors.claim_boundary: The product must not act as a customs broker or imply a broker relationship/signoff.
research_anchors.usable_fact: Licensed broker routing is the human-review path for customs broker work and importer responsibility boundaries.

research_anchors.claim_boundary: Sanctions screening claims require dated searches and reviewer signoff before action.
research_anchors.usable_fact: Global Affairs Canada is the sanctions/restricted-country routing source for Canada-focused screening warnings.

research_anchors.claim_boundary: A listed importer is a possible lead only; it is not buyer validation.
research_anchors.usable_fact: The Canadian Importers Database is a lead-discovery route for companies importing goods into Canada.

research_anchors.claim_boundary: India export permission, licensing, incentives, and product-specific compliance require current source review and qualified advice.
research_anchors.source_name: India Directorate General of Foreign Trade
research_anchors.usable_fact: DGFT is the India origin export-policy route for IEC, export/import policy, certificates, SCOMET, trade stats, and export management topics.

research_anchors.claim_boundary: Global trade data supports research signals only; it does not prove buyer demand, margin, or compliance.
research_anchors.source_name: World Bank World Integrated Trade Solution
research_anchors.usable_fact: WITS is a route for international merchandise trade, tariff, and non-tariff measures data.

research_anchors.claim_boundary: Trade Map supports research and lead routing only; it does not validate demand or buyers.
research_anchors.usable_fact: Trade Map is a route for import/export values, volumes, growth, market share, and company-directory research.

research_anchors.claim_boundary: HS candidate generation is not classification confirmation.
research_anchors.usable_fact: The HS is the global commodity-description and coding route for HS candidate logic.

research_anchors.claim_boundary: Responsibility-path guidance requires user confirmation and, where needed, broker/legal review.
research_anchors.usable_fact: Incoterms are the responsibility-path route for tasks, costs, and risks between sellers and buyers.

research_anchors.claim_boundary: AI safety controls require implementation evidence and review before real document use.
research_anchors.usable_fact: Indirect prompt injection can come from external files or websites and can manipulate outputs, leak information, or affect decisions.
